title: श्री वासुदेव स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
वासुदेवं परं ब्रह्म सर्वकारणकारणम्।
सर्वभूतान्तरात्मानं प्रणमामि जगत्पतिम्॥१॥

वसत्यस्मिन् जगत्सर्वं यः स वासुदेवो विभुः।
सर्वगः सर्वभूतेषु तस्मै वासुदेवाय ते नमः॥२॥

वसुदेवसुतं देवं कंसचाणूरमर्दनम्।
देवकीपरमानन्दं वासुदेवं नमाम्यहम्॥३॥

वासुदेवः परं ज्योतिर्वासुदेवः परा गतिः।
वासुदेवः परं धाम वासुदेवाय ते नमः॥४॥

सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।
इत्युक्तवान् यो भगवान् वासुदेवं भजामहे॥५॥

वासुदेवस्सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः।
ज्ञानवान् मां प्रपद्यन्ते इत्युक्तः श्रीहरिः स्वयम्॥६॥

यस्मात्परतरं नास्ति यस्मात्परतरं नहि।
सर्वकारणकारणं वासुदेवं नमामि तम्॥७॥

अनादिं च परं ब्रह्म निर्गुणं गुणभासकम्।
वासुदेवमजं शाश्वतं पुराणपुरुषोत्तमम्॥८॥

वासुदेवस्तवं पुण्यं यः पठेत् प्रयतः सदा।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो वैकुण्ठं स गमिष्यति॥

॥ इति श्री वासुदेव स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
Vasudevam Param Brahma Sarvakarana-Karanam.
Sarvabhutantaratmanam Pranamami Jagatpatim.||1||

Vasatyasmin Jagatsarvam Yah Sa Vasudevo Vibhuh.
Sarvagah Sarvabhuteshu Tasmai Vasudevaya Te Namah.||2||

Vasudevasutam Devam Kamsachanura-Mardanam.
Devakiparamanandaam Vasudevam Namamyaham.||3||

Vasudevah Param Jyotir Vasudevah Para Gatih.
Vasudevah Param Dhama Vasudevaya Te Namah.||4||

Sarvadharmaan Parityajya Mamekam Sharanam Vraja.
Ityuktavan Yo Bhagavan Vasudevam Bhajaamahe.||5||

Vasudevassarvam-Iti Sa Mahatma Sudurlabhah.
Jnanavan Mam Prapadyante Ityuktah Shriharih Svayam.||6||

Yasmat Parataram Nasti Yasmat Parataram Nahi.
Sarvakarana-Karanam Vasudevam Namami Tam.||7||

Anadim Cha Param Brahma Nirgunam Gunabhasakam.
Vasudevam Ajam Shashvatam Puranapurushottamam.||8||

Vasudevastavam Punyam Yah Pathet Prayatah Sada.
Sarvapapavinirmukto Vaikuntham Sa Gamishyati.

|| Iti Shri Vasudeva Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
परम ब्रह्म वासुदेव, सर्व कारणों के कारण, सर्व भूतों के अन्तरात्मा, जगत्पति को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ।

जिनमें समस्त जगत वास करता है, वे विभु वासुदेव, सर्वगत, सर्व भूतों में विद्यमान — उन वासुदेव को नमस्कार।

वसुदेव पुत्र, कंस-चाणूर मर्दन करने वाले, देवकी के परमानन्द वासुदेव को मैं नमस्कार करता हूँ।

वासुदेव परम ज्योति हैं, परम गति हैं, परम धाम हैं — वासुदेव को नमस्कार।

सब धर्मों को छोड़कर मेरी एक शरण में आओ — ऐसा कहने वाले भगवान वासुदेव को हम भजते हैं।

'वासुदेवः सर्वम्' — ऐसा जानने वाला महात्मा अत्यन्त दुर्लभ है। ज्ञानवान मेरी शरण लेते हैं — ऐसा स्वयं श्रीहरि ने कहा है।

जिनसे परे कुछ नहीं, जिनसे श्रेष्ठ कोई नहीं — सर्व कारणों के कारण वासुदेव को मैं नमन करता हूँ।

अनादि, परम ब्रह्म, निर्गुण फिर भी गुणों को प्रकाशित करने वाले, अज, शाश्वत, पुराणपुरुषोत्तम वासुदेव।

जो सदा पवित्र भाव से वासुदेव स्तव पढ़ता है, वह सर्व पापों से मुक्त होकर वैकुण्ठ गमन करता है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीवासुदेवस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य वेदव्यास ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीवासुदेव देवता। ॐ नमो भगवते वासुदेवाय बीजम्। वैकुण्ठप्राप्तिशक्तिः। सर्वपापमोचनं कीलकम्। श्रीवासुदेवप्रीत्यर्थे पाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Reveals the supreme nature of Vasudeva as the cause of all causes
Contains key Gita teachings on surrender to the divine
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and purifies the devotee completely
Invokes deep devotion and understanding of vasudeva tattva
Leads to Vaikuntha (the supreme abode of Vishnu)
Ideal for daily recitation and philosophical meditation
description: Vasudeva Stotram (श्री वासुदेव स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Vishnu. Drawn from Bhagavata Purana and Bhagavad Gita, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to lead to Vaikuntha (the supreme abode of Vishnu) as well as grant deep devotion and understanding of Vasudeva Tattva. Additionally, it is known to contain key Gita teachings on surrender to the divine. For best results, devotees chant this on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Ekadashi, Gita Jayanti, and Vaikuntha Ekadashi. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 5 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Vasudeva Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vasudeva Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Hymn to Lord Vasudeva as the supreme cause of all, with Gita wisdom for devotion and liberation.
relatedSearches: Vasudeva sutam devam benefits daily recitation

seoFaqs.question: Which composition is most commonly meant by Vasudeva Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The most widely circulated text titled Vasudeva Stotram is the Krishna Ashtakam attributed to Adi Shankaracharya, beginning with the famous opening Vasudeva Sutam Devam Kamsa Chanura Mardanam Devaki Paramanandam Krishnam Vande Jagad-Gurum. The hymn praises Krishna explicitly as the son of Vasudeva, identifying him through his earthly parentage and his cosmic role. A second text bearing the same name is the Vasudeva Stuti from the Bhagavata Purana 10.3, where Vasudeva and Devaki praise the newborn Krishna in the Mathura prison. Most printed manuals and contemporary recitation videos default to the Shankaracharya Krishnashtakam when titled Vasudeva Stotram. (Sources: JKYog Vasudeva Sutam Devam; Shlokam Krishnashtakam; Bhaktinidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the term Vasudeva mean and to whom does it refer?
seoFaqs.answer: Vasudeva carries two distinct senses in Vaishnava theology. As a patronymic, Vasudeva refers to Krishna as the son of Vasudeva, the Yadava prince who became Krishna's earthly father. As a theological epithet, Vasudeva (or Vaasudeva) is one of the four chief vyuhas in Pancharatra Vaishnavism, denoting the supreme aspect of Vishnu from which Sankarshana, Pradyumna and Aniruddha emanate; in this sense, Vasudeva is the transcendental Krishna himself. The Bhagavad Gita 7.19 famously states Vasudevah sarvam iti, meaning the wise realise everything is Vasudeva. Vasudeva Stotram operates on both registers, addressing the historical son of Vasudeva and the cosmic Vasudeva in the same breath. (Sources: JKYog; Shlokam; Hindu Devotional Blog Vasudeva Sutam.)

seoFaqs.question: Why are Kamsa and Chanura mentioned in the very first verse?
seoFaqs.answer: The first verse compresses the entire arc of Krishna's Mathura mission into one shloka by naming the four key figures: his parents Vasudeva and Devaki and his two principal adversaries Kamsa, the maternal uncle and tyrant king of Mathura, and Chanura, the formidable royal wrestler whom Krishna defeated in the arena before slaying Kamsa. By referencing the deliverance of his parents from the Mathura prison and the destruction of the wicked, the verse establishes Krishna's identity as both the loving son and the world-redeeming Jagad-Guru. Each of the remaining seven verses then expands a different aspect of his persona, building from this foundational shloka. (Sources: Shlokam Krishnashtakam; Hindu Devotional Blog; Bhaktinidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Vasudeva Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn is recited daily by Krishna devotees as part of morning and evening puja, and is especially emphasised on Krishna Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami), Rohini Nakshatra days each month, all Ekadashi observances, the festivals of Holi and Govardhan Puja, and during the Kartika maasa devotional month. Iskcon and Madhva temples include the Krishnashtakam in their evening sandhya-arati. Some Pushti-Marg households of the Vallabha sampradaya recite it as the closing prayer after Yashoda-bhog and before the night-time shayan-arati. The compact eight-verse length (under three minutes) makes it suitable for repeated recitation in 8, 16, 21 or 108 cycles on Janmashtami night. (Sources: Drikpanchang; Bhaktinidhi; JKYog.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the spiritual benefits attributed to Vasudeva Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Adi Shankaracharya's phalashruti at the close of the Krishnashtakam states that one who recites these eight verses in the morning with devotion attains the merit of all the Vedas, becomes free from the cycle of rebirth, and ultimately reaches the lotus feet of Krishna. Practical benefits enumerated by traditional commentators include removal of mental anxiety, calming of household disputes, protection of newborn children when chanted near them, success in education for students who recite before study, and the cultivation of bhakti-rasa. The hymn is particularly recommended for couples seeking the blessing of children, given its vivid imagery of Krishna's divine birth and his parents' joy at his appearance. (Sources: Shlokam Krishnashtakam; JKYog; Hindu Devotional Blog.)

seoFaqs.question: Is Vasudeva Stotram different from Vasudeva Stuti in the Bhagavata Purana?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes, though both share the name and central theme. Vasudeva Stotram in popular use refers to the Adi Shankara Krishnashtakam beginning Vasudeva Sutam Devam, and is a devotional praise hymn meant for daily recitation. Vasudeva Stuti, found at Bhagavata Purana 10.3.13-31, is the longer prayer spoken by Vasudeva himself when the newborn Krishna manifests in his four-armed cosmic form within the Mathura prison cell, and is technically called the Sutiki Stuti since it occurs at Krishna's birth (suti). The Stuti is more philosophical and recounts Vishnu's cosmic functions, while the Stotram is more iconographic and emotive. Devotees often recite both on Janmashtami night, beginning with the Stuti at midnight and the Stotram at dawn. (Sources: Hindu Devotional Blog Vasudeva Sutham; Shlokam.)
